titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Gerrard to Augustine Birrell, 29 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas M. Gerrard

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Thomas M. Gerrard, a Dublin-based solicitor and solicitor for the Royal College of Surgeons, to Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), chief secretary of Ireland, for the position of chief Crown solicitor for Ireland, following the death of Sir Malachy Kelly.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5858/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Letter to Rt Hon Augustine Birrell P.C.,M.P. page 1 25 Westmoreland Street, Dublin 29th March 1916 Sir, I have the honour to request that I may be appointed to the position
of Chief Crown Solicitor for Ireland rendered vacant by the death of Sir
Malachy J Kelly. I was admitted a Solicitor on the 14th December 1895
and since that date have practised in partnership with Mr Charles Daly
under the name of Thomas Gerrard & Co in Dublin and at Drogheda County Louth My firm is well known in Dublin and throughout Ireland and has conducted
many important cases. I am also Solicitor for the Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland. I
am the second son of the late Thomas Gerrard Crown Solicitor for Queens
County & Carlow. I am of course over military age. All the remaining
members of my family have served or are serving with the Navy or Army. My
eldest brother Dr P N Gerrard, Captain Malay States Rifles Commandant of
the German prisoners camp at Signapore Straits Settlement was killed while
notifying the Town of Signapore of the mutiny of the Indian troops during
the war. My next brother J.D. Gerrard, who is a Resident Magistrate, is at
present serving with the Army Services Corp. My next brother Capt. H V
Gerrard, Second Border Regiment was killed with the Seventh Division at
the first battle of Ypres and my remaining brother Lieut. Col. & L Gerrard
is a wing Commander in the Royal Naval Air Service; my only nephew is
a Flight Lieutenant in the Royal Navel Air service. I was born on the 14th December 1872. I am Sir,
Your obedient servant Thomas Gerrard
The Right Hon. Augustine Birrell P.C. M.P. Chief Secretary of Ireland Dublin Castle
